name: GR 123: Tour of Biscay
name_original: Monte Serantes desde Neguri — ida y vuelta por el río Nervión
description: Hike Monte Serantes from Neguri for 9.9 miles (15.9 km) with 1453 feet (443 metres) elevation gain, offering panoramic views of Bilbao and t
region_intro: Hiking around Getxo, a coastal town on the right bank of the Bilbao Estuary, offers diverse natural landscapes and historical attractions. The region is characterized by dramatic cliffs, golden beaches, and charming harbors. These features provide a variety of scenic trails for different fitness levels, integrating natural beauty with historical landmarks. Best hiking trails around Getxo The most popular hiking route is GR 123: Tour of Biscay, a 9.8 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate path offers varied terrain as it traverses the region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Punta Galea Cliffs – Gorrondatxe Beach loop from Ibarbengoa, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This route features the dramatic Punta Galea Cliffs and the unique Flysch rock formations at Gorrondatxe Beach. Local hikers also love the Arriluce Lighthouse – Puerto Viejo de Algorta loop from Areeta, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the historic Old Port of Algorta, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Getxo is defined by coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and historical landmarks like the Aixerrota Windmill. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging hikes. The routes in Getxo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Getxo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain and difficulty level of hiking trails in Getxo?
A: Hiking trails in Getxo primarily feature coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and varied terrain that can include paved sections, woods, and open spaces. The difficulty levels range from easy walks suitable for families to moderate and difficult hikes requiring good fitness. Overall, there are over 80 routes, with about 45 easy, 38 moderate, and a few difficult options.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Getxo?
A: Getxo's hiking trails offer stunning natural features like the dramatic Punta Galea Cliffs, golden beaches such as Gorrondatxe (Azkorri) and Arrigunaga, and unique Flysch rock formations, particularly visible at Gorrondatxe Beach. You can also explore historical landmarks like the 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the Aixerrota Windmill, and La Galea Fort.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Getxo?
A: Yes, Getxo offers numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed stroll. Many coastal paths are well-maintained and provide scenic views without significant elevation changes. For example, the area around Ereaga Beach and the Old Port of Algorta offers pleasant, flat walks.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Getxo?
A: For experienced trekkers, Getxo does offer more challenging options. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20509123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Galea Cliffs – Txitxarropunta Cliffs loop from Elexalde Getxo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 21 km and involves significant elevation changes, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience with extensive cliffside views.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Getxo?
A: Absolutely. Many of Getxo's trails are suitable for families, especially the easier coastal walks and those around the beaches. These routes often feature flat terrain and offer opportunities to explore charming areas like the Old Port of Algorta or enjoy the sandy shores of Ereaga Beach. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it's appropriate for your family's fitness level.

Q: Are Getxo's hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: While many outdoor areas in Getxo are generally welcoming to dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas, near wildlife, or on beaches where restrictions might apply during certain seasons. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other hikers and local residents.

Q: Are there circular walking routes available in Getxo?
A: Yes, Getxo features several excellent circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location, making logistics easier. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20505777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Galea Cliffs – Gorrondatxe Beach loop from Ibarbengoa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers dramatic coastal scenery, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriluce Lighthouse – Puerto Viejo de Algorta loop from Areeta&lt;/a&gt;, combining coastal views with historical charm.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I visit near the hiking trails?
A: Getxo's hiking trails often pass by or are close to significant historical sites. You can visit the 18th-century Aixerrota Windmill and La Galea Fort along the coastal walk. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is also easily accessible and offers panoramic views from its pedestrian walkway. The charming Old Port of Algorta is another picturesque historical area to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Getxo?
A: Getxo is a great hiking destination year-round, but the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking. Temperatures are mild, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, ideal for combining hikes with beach visits, while winter offers cooler, crisp air, though some coastal paths might be exposed to wind.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Getxo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the dramatic cliffs, and the unique blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks. The variety of trails catering to different fitness levels and the well-maintained paths are also often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near Getxo's hiking trails?
A: Getxo is well-connected by public transport, particularly the Bilbao Metro, which has several stops within the town, making many trailheads accessible. For those driving, parking is generally available in various areas of Getxo, especially near popular beaches and attractions, though it can be busier during peak season. It's advisable to check specific parking options for your chosen trailhead.

Q: How long do hiking routes in Getxo typically take to complete?
A: Hiking routes in Getxo vary significantly in length and duration. Shorter, easy walks around areas like the Old Port of Algorta might take an hour or two. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arriluce Lighthouse – Puerto Viejo de Algorta loop from Areeta&lt;/a&gt; (8 km), can be completed in about 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309636063/gr-123-tour-of-biscay" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 123: Tour of Biscay&lt;/a&gt; (15.9 km) can take around 4-5 hours.
tips: The monument is intended to depict the dramatic struggle of the city of Bilbao in the form of a man with the sea in the form of Poseidon. Poseidon is already lying on the ground with his trident broken.

The Vizcaya bridge, also known as the Portugalete suspension bridge, is an emblematic engineering work from the end of the 19th century. In its day it was the first transporter bridge in the world, a milestone that, together with its beautiful iron style, led to its being declared a World Heritage Site.
Small mountain with cow pastures, which are freely accessible. In the evening you can watch a great sunset!

Serantes is a very beautiful and easily accessible mountain, the view is great when the air is clear (on one side the Spanish countryside and on the other side the port of Bilbao + Atlantic coast). However, some paths are a bit overgrown, so watch out for ticks and snakes!

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the Monte Serantes from Neguri tour is accessible by public transport, making it convenient to reach without a car. Specific parking information is not detailed, but public transport is a viable option.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is categorized as moderate, meaning a good level of fitness is required. While many paths are accessible, some sections demand sure-footedness due to varied terrain and potentially steep or slippery parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including approximately 5.03 km of sidewalk, 3.58 km of path, 3.39 km of hiking trail, and 2.15 km of road. You can expect about 7.75 km of paved ground, 3.13 km of unpaved sections, and 2.71 km of asphalt. Some parts can be steep, and care should be taken on dusty downhill sections.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The hike offers fantastic views, especially from the summit of Monte Serantes. You'll see the Spanish countryside on one side and the bustling port of Bilbao and the Atlantic coast on the other. From the View from Mirador de San Juan (Monte Serantes), you get a 360-degree panoramic view of the Great Bilbao and the El Abra Bay.

faqs.question: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers several notable features. You can visit the ruins of the Serantes Fort and the restored Serantes Tower, built in 1881, which offers excellent views and is open to visitors on weekends. You may also see the Vizcaya Bridge (Puente de Vizcaya), a UNESCO World Heritage site.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not explicitly state rules regarding dogs. However, as a natural trail with varied terrain, it is generally suitable for well-behaved dogs on a leash, but always check local regulations before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but it's always wise to check the weather forecast. There's a local saying: 'if Monte Serantes has a txapela (fog covering its summit), it's a sign of rain,' so be prepared for changing conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike this trail or access the Monte Serantes area.

faqs.question: How long does this hike typically take?
faqs.answer: The total length of a route to Serantes can be around 10 kilometers, taking approximately 4 hours. This duration includes time for breaks and photos, but the specific route described here is longer at nearly 16 km, so expect a longer duration, around 4 hours and 40 minutes of moving time according to tour data.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'Bizkaiko bira' trail (94%) and also passes through sections of '[CIMA BI09] Serantes' (41%) and 'Areetako kaia' (18%).

faqs.question: Is a ferry crossing required for this round trip?
faqs.answer: Yes, an interesting aspect of this round trip via the Nervión river is that it may require a ferry crossing as part of the route.

waypoints.name: View from Mirador de San Juan (Monte Serantes)
